$comment: Source of truth for release note generation. Edit here, never in scripts. Requires PR + ADR to change.

modelRouting.$comment: Claude model per task. Change here only — never in scripts.

reasoning.$comment: Opus for judgment, editorial quality, cross-repo reasoning, security, tone unification

validation.$comment: Haiku for pattern-matching validation and internal section stripping

pipelineOrder.$comment: Canonical execution order for Phase 6. Change here only.

versioning.$comment: Agent proposes; human confirms before any file is written.
versioning.patch: improvements, bug-fixes, or token/component changes only
versioning.proposalFormat: State current version, proposed version, bump type, and the specific changes driving it. Wait for confirmation.

crossRepoImpact.$comment: When a change in one repo affects consumers of the other.
triggers: token removed or renamed that is referenced in rei-cedar component styles
CSS custom property removed from dist that rei-cedar components consume
TypeScript type removed that rei-cedar re-exports
import path changed that rei-cedar references internally

hard co-release dependency: one package version requires a specific version of the other to function — both versions must ship together and the release notes for each must name the required counterpart version explicitly

coRelease.$comment: Use when two packages must ship together. Agent surfaces this prominently in overview and be-aware-of — not buried in cross-repo notes.

fields.requires: string — the counterpart package and version (e.g. '@rei/cedar@17')
fields.reason: string — one sentence on why they are coupled
fields.upgradeOrder: string | null — if order matters, state it explicitly

consumerSurface.$comment: Applied before any analysis. Filtered items never appear in any output.
counts: CSS custom property names in dist
ignore: features added in alpha and reverted before stable — these must not appear in stable release notes and must appear in be-aware-of only if consumers may have depended on the alpha behavior
consumerSurface.breakingOnlyIf: the change alters what a consumer imports, references, or relies on

humanSections.$comment: Omit empty sections. Never write empty headers.

conditionallyRequired.be-aware-of: only if release-intent.md BOLO is not 'None.'

stripFromOutput: any section containing only file path lists

breakingChangeSubBulletFormat.$comment: Sub-bullets under breaking changes must use bold labels. Enforce during unifyDraftTone pass.

machineRecord.$comment: One record per discrete consumer-surface change.

fields.changeType: breaking | feature | improvement | fix | token | api | deprecation
fields.category: token | component | build | docs | tooling | config
fields.subject: string
fields.resolvedValue: { before: string, after: string } | null — actual CSS/JS values
fields.consumerActionRequired: boolean — computed from breaking + migrationSteps + crossRepoImpact
fields.visualImpact: color | spacing | typography | motion | radius | none
fields.figmaAction: { type: update-value | rename-variable | none, variableName, before, after } | null
fields.commitSource: string | null — internal only; scrubbed in external publish
fields.agentNote: string | null — internal only; scrubbed in external publish

releaseNotesConvention.$comment: Release notes are additive. Never modify or replace existing files.
rules: Major releases: default: true — shown by default in Storybook
Minor and patch releases: default: false — additive, accessible but do not replace the major
Never set default: false on an existing file when adding a new one
Include full semver in the filename for patches and minors (e.g. cedar-tokens-14.0.1.md)
Major versions omit patch/minor in the filename (e.g. cedar-tokens-14.md)

frontMatterTemplate.default: true for major, false for minor/patch

releaseIndex.$comment: Append-only. Never rewrite existing records. Corrections use corrects pointer.

recordFields.machineJSON: string — path to archived scrubbed JSON
recordFields.next: string | null — filled on next release

rules.match: published token name removed from dist
published token name changed in dist
token meaning or intended usage changed
component prop removed from public API
component prop renamed in public API
prop default changed in consumer-visible way
CSS custom property removed from published dist
DOM structure change breaking consumer selectors
published import path or export removed

notBreaking: internal refactors with no consumer-visible effect
tooling or config changes with no output change
features added in alpha and fully reverted before stable — treat as never shipped; add be-aware-of entry only if the alpha was public and consumers may have built against it
